commands.author: armsteadj1

target.title: Refresh MCP OAuth auth-profile tokens
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Remote MCP servers such as Ducktape can be configured with static `Authorization` headers today. That makes an otherwise refresh-capable OAuth credential decay into dead bearer text, so tool discovery/calls fail once the access token expires.

This PR lets OpenClaw own that refresh path for remote MCP servers by binding a server to an existing refresh-capable auth profile:

```json
{
  "mcp": {
    "servers": {
      "ducktape": {
        "url": "https://agents.ducktape.xyz/mcp",
        "transport": "streamable-http",
        "auth": "oauth",
        "oauth": {
          "authProfileId": "ducktape:mcp"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Summary

- Added `mcp.servers.*.oauth.authProfileId` to config schema/types/help/labels/docs.
- Added an MCP auth-profile resolver that:
  - loads the selected auth profile,
  - requires it to be a refreshable OAuth profile,
  - reuses `resolveApiKeyForProfile` for refresh/locking/store semantics,
  - strips stale static `Authorization` headers,
  - never projects refresh tokens downstream.
- Wired auth-profile bearer injection into:
  - embedded/session MCP HTTP transports,
  - CLI bundle MCP projection via per-server token env vars,
  - native Codex app-server user MCP projection.
- Redacted literal app-server Authorization headers before computing the user-MCP resume fingerprint, while retaining a one-way `sha256` lifecycle marker so bearer changes rotate the Codex app-server MCP binding instead of reusing an old client.
- Preserved agent-local auth scope for session MCP runtimes by carrying `agentDir` through runtime creation and transport resolution, and by splitting runtime reuse when the same session/config is invoked under a different agent directory.

## Related Issues / Context

Fixes #91585.

James linked the Ducktape MCP expired-token failure and asked OpenClaw to own token refresh instead of relying on a James-specific cron or static config rewrite.

This keeps existing MCP-native OAuth login behavior unchanged: `auth: "oauth"` without `oauth.authProfileId` still uses the MCP SDK OAuth provider and `openclaw mcp login`.

## Example / Behavior Sketch

- `auth: "oauth"` + no `oauth.authProfileId`:
  - existing MCP OAuth flow remains active;
  - static `Authorization` headers are still ignored on OAuth servers.
- `auth: "oauth"` + `oauth.authProfileId`:
  - OpenClaw resolves the auth profile before runtime projection;
  - static/stale `Authorization` is removed;
  - downstream MCP config gets only the current bearer access token/header;
  - static `token` profiles fail clearly instead of being treated as refreshable.

## Evidence

Focused tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/mcp-auth-profile.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.codex.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.user-config.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts
```

Result: 4 Vitest shards passed, covering 98 tests.

Follow-up ClawSweeper repair tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/mcp-auth-profile.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
```

Result: passed. This includes a regression test proving session MCP runtime reuse is split by `agentDir`, so a runtime built for `/agents/one` is not reused for `/agents/two`.

Tool-filter repair tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts src/agents/mcp-auth-profile.test.ts
```

Result: passed. Coverage now proves exact OpenClaw `toolFilter.include` / `toolFilter.exclude` values project into Codex-native `enabled_tools` / `disabled_tools`, auth-profile-backed runtime projection preserves those filters while injecting the fresh bearer, and wildcard filters fail closed with a clear Codex projection error instead of exposing all remote tools.

Broader post-filter focused pack:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/mcp-auth-profile.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
```

Result: passed, covering the session runtime, auth-profile resolver, Codex projection, app-server lifecycle, and SDK surface report tests together.

Lint / type / whitespace:

```text
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/mcp-auth-profile.ts src/agents/mcp-auth-profile.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-types.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/codex-mcp-config.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.ts src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/codex-mcp-projection.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.user-mcp-servers.test.ts src/config/types.mcp.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/config/schema.help.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs
```

Result: passed.

```text
corepack pnpm tsgo:test:src
```

Result: passed.

```text
git diff --check
```

Result: passed.

```text
node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check
```

Result: passed with public package SDK budgets `10383` exports / `5212` callable exports, matching the intentional public projection helper added by this PR.

Lifecycle regression proof added for the ClawSweeper stale-binding finding:

- New test: `startOrResumeThread — user mcp.servers projection` now starts a Codex app-server thread with `Authorization: Bearer access-token-one`, then reruns with `Authorization: Bearer access-token-two`.
- Expected result after fix: OpenClaw sends `thread/start`, not `thread/resume`, so the Codex MCP client/binding is rebuilt for the new bearer.
- Secret-safety assertion: the stored `userMcpServersFingerprint` contains only `<redacted:sha256:...>` and does not contain either raw access token.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: auth-profile-backed MCP servers no longer project stale static Authorization headers; OpenClaw resolves the refresh-capable OAuth profile and passes only the current bearer token downstream.
- Real environment tested: local macOS checkout on the rebased PR branch, Node 24, temp OpenClaw auth-profile SQLite store, temp localhost HTTP MCP proof server.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` proof script importing `resolveMcpAuthProfileBundleConfig` and `withMcpAuthProfileBearer` from this branch, writing a temp OAuth auth profile, starting a localhost MCP proof server, projecting the MCP config, and calling the server through both the projected CLI config and same-origin wrapped fetch path.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
MCP OAuth auth-profile runtime proof
authProfileId: proof:mcp
stored profile type: oauth / refresh-capable: true
input configured Authorization: Bearer <redacted-stale-access>
projected auth field present: false
projected oauth field present: false
projected Authorization header: Bearer ${OPENCLAW_MCP_AUTH_C1CDA2636282_TOKEN}
projected env token redacted: <redacted-current-access>
refresh token projected downstream: false
local MCP proof server status: 200
local MCP proof server saw Authorization: Bearer <redacted-current-access>
local MCP proof server exposed tools: proof.echo
same-origin wrapped fetch status: 200
same-origin wrapped fetch exposed tools: proof.echo
same-origin wrapped fetch saw Authorization: Bearer <redacted-current-access>
```

- Observed result after fix: the downstream MCP client config has no OpenClaw-only `auth`/`oauth` fields, no refresh token, no stale Authorization value, and the proof server exposes `proof.echo` only when it receives the current OAuth-profile bearer.
- What was not tested: this proof uses a local refresh-capable OAuth profile and local MCP server, not live Ducktape credentials. No real third-party tokens are included.

## Remaining Limitations

- This PR adds the generic OpenClaw-owned refresh/projection path, but it does not mutate any live operator config such as Hedwig's current Ducktape server entry.
- Live Ducktape proof still requires a real `ducktape:mcp` style OAuth auth profile binding in config. No real credentials are included in this branch or PR.

target.title: perf(agents): cache provider tool schema normalization
target.body:
## Summary
- Cache provider-normalized tool schema `parameters` for repeated embedded agent turns, but only when OpenClaw can identify one of its bundled SDK tool-compat normalizers.
- Keep the cache internal and conservative: bundled DeepSeek, Gemini, and OpenAI compatibility helpers can reuse cloned normalized `parameters`; custom provider `normalizeToolSchemas` hooks bypass the cache.
- Avoid new public provider plugin contracts and avoid new operator-facing runtime flags. Tool objects, execute closures, approval state, guards, and runtime policy are still rebuilt per turn.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated embedded agent turns can avoid redoing bundled provider tool-schema compatibility normalization when the provider/model/api/schema signature is unchanged. Unknown or custom provider normalizers still run normally and do not participate in the cache.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `tool-schema-cache-pr3`, current PR head `dee9bc5aa9e603be72a640b3483ae669ade3c29e`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a terminal proof with `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against the production `normalizeProviderToolSchemas` entry. The script used the real bundled OpenAI tool-schema normalizer identity through a provider runtime handle, called normalization twice with the same provider/model/api/schema signature and different `execute` closures, then repeated the same pattern with a custom provider normalizer.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the current head:

```json
{
  "head": "dee9bc5aa9e603be72a640b3483ae669ade3c29e",
  "bundledCompat": {
    "afterFirst": { "bypass": 0, "hit": 0, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1 },
    "afterSecond": { "bypass": 0, "hit": 1, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1 },
    "cacheHitIncreasedOnSecondNormalize": true,
    "normalizedParametersEqual": true,
    "secondRunExecuteClosurePreserved": true
  },
  "customProvider": {
    "customNormalizeCalls": 2,
    "afterCustom": { "bypass": 2, "hit": 0, "miss": 0, "store": 0, "size": 0 },
    "customProviderBypassedCache": true
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix: The bundled OpenAI compatibility path produced one miss and store on the first normalization, then one cache hit on the second normalization while preserving the fresh second `execute` closure. The custom provider path bypassed the cache twice and invoked its normalizer twice, proving the cache remains internal to known bundled compatibility helpers.

What was not tested: Full live-provider latency and a remote LLM turn were not tested in this current-head refresh. This PR caches only provider-normalized schema `parameters`; it does not cache complete tool inventory/materialization, policy resolution, prompt inputs, or full agent-turn startup.

## Additional validation

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime-errors.test.ts
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 10.92s
Test files: 6 passed
Tests: 124 passed

pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 <changed files>
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD && git diff --check
exit 0
```

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Repeated embedded-agent turns reuse bundled provider tool-schema compatibility normalization for the same provider/model/api/schema signature, while non-bundled/custom provider normalizers still bypass the cache so plugin-specific behavior is not hidden.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout at PR head `b183b3fbe3379f870b8a65e5bf2fe43c8e5aa2ca`; production `normalizeProviderToolSchemas` entry point; real bundled OpenAI normalizer `normalizeOpenAIToolSchemas` via runtime handle; separate custom runtime handle for the non-bundled provider path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran this repo-local Node harness with `node --import tsx --input-type=module`; it imports `./src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts` and `./src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.ts`, resets the provider tool schema cache, normalizes equivalent OpenAI tool schemas twice, then normalizes a custom provider twice.
- Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "head": "b183b3fbe3379f870b8a65e5bf2fe43c8e5aa2ca",
  "bundledCompat": {
    "afterFirst": {
      "bypass": 0,
      "hit": 0,
      "miss": 1,
      "store": 1,
      "size": 1
    },
    "afterSecond": {
      "bypass": 0,
      "hit": 1,
      "miss": 1,
      "store": 1,
      "size": 1
    },
    "cacheHitIncreasedOnSecondNormalize": true,
    "normalizedParametersEqual": true,
    "secondRunExecuteClosurePreserved": true
  },
  "customProvider": {
    "customNormalizeCalls": 2,
    "afterCustom": {
      "bypass": 2,
      "hit": 0,
      "miss": 0,
      "store": 0,
      "size": 0
    },
    "customProviderBypassedCache": true
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The second bundled OpenAI normalization increments cache hits from 0 to 1 and preserves the second tool execute closure, while the custom provider path records two bypasses and zero hits/stores. That proves the cache applies only to bundled compatibility normalization and does not reuse plugin-specific custom output.
- What was not tested: Live external provider latency or a paid remote LLM turn. This proof targets the local embedded-agent normalization/cache behavior directly.
